name: ui_module_definition
purpose: Design UI modules and pages for the application â€” derived from user tasks, use cases, business functions, and domain entities. Produces a structured UI module catalog and per-module page specifications ready for UI source code generation.

menu.label: string â€” nav menu label
menu.icon: string â€” icon name hint e.g. calendar, users, clipboard
menu.route: full route id of the module entry point â€” e.g. /my-leave/dashboard

modules.name: string â€” human-readable module name
modules.icon: string â€” icon name hint e.g. calendar, users, clipboard
modules.purpose: one sentence â€” user task this module supports
backendModules: Mnn â€” backend module IDs this UI module reads/writes

pages.name: string
pages.pattern: dashboard | list | detail | form | wizard | modal | split-view
pages.purpose: what the user achieves on this page
requirementRefs: FR-nn or BR-nn â€” requirements this page fulfills
pages.businessFunctionRef: BF-nn.nn â€” business function driving this page
requiredRoles: actor names who can access this page
shows: data items or summary cards displayed

actions.label: button/link label
actions.type: navigate | submit | delete | confirm | filter | export | modal | bulk
requiredRoles: actor names â€” omit if same as page
actions.navigatesTo: full route id e.g. /my-leave/:id or null

pages.emptyState: message shown when the page has no data
drillsInto: full route id â€” pages reachable from this page, e.g. /my-leave/:id

subNav.label: string â€” tab or secondary menu label
subNav.note: include only when the module has 3+ peer sections worth a persistent tab bar; omit otherwise

dashboard.purpose: Overview of key metrics and entry points â€” for actors who need a summary before acting
dashboard.avoid: editing data directly on a dashboard

list.purpose: Browse, search, and filter a collection â€” entry point for detail or form

detail.purpose: Read all fields of a single record plus related items
detail.avoid: inline editing unless UX specifically requires it

form.purpose: Create or edit a single record â€” always paired with a list or detail page
form.avoid: forms with more than 15 fields â€” split into wizard instead

wizard.purpose: Multi-step process where later steps depend on earlier answers
wizard.avoid: wizards for simple forms â€” only use when steps have conditional branching

modal.purpose: Lightweight confirmation or short form that does not warrant a full page navigation
modal.avoid: modals with more than 5 fields or nested navigation

split-view.purpose: Master list on left, detail or form on right â€” for high-density workflows

naming_rules.module_id: UI-M01, UI-M02 â€” sequential, prefixed UI-M
naming_rules.page_id: Full route path â€” routePrefix + routePath concatenated, e.g. /my-leave/dashboard, /my-leave/:id/edit. Use the exact route as the page id. This makes every reference self-documenting and collision-free across modules.

rules: Read application.json for actors â€” all primaryUsers and requiredRoles must reference defined actors
Read use-case files â€” each use case must map to at least one page
Design from user tasks, not database tables â€” derive pages from what actors need to accomplish
Every page must declare requirementRefs and businessFunctionRef â€” no untraced pages
Prefer dashboard, list, detail, and form patterns â€” use wizard only for branching multi-step flows
Keep navigation shallow â€” routePrefix + one sub-path level maximum
Each drill-down must appear in both drillsInto on the source page and navigation.flows
Actions of type delete must be type confirm â€” never a direct destructive action
Filters only where data volume or search need is identified in requirements
Validation rules required on all form pages â€” must align with DomainValidator constraints where applicable
RequiredRoles must map directly to the Actors defined in the requirements baseline
emptyState required on all list and dashboard pages
backendModules must reference module IDs from module-catalog.json
relatedEntities must reference entity names from entity files
Priority: core = required for MVP, secondary = important but deferrable, optional = nice-to-have
One UI module per cohesive user workflow â€” do not split a single workflow across modules
No new requirements â€” all pages must be traceable to requirements or use cases
ui-catalog.json must include a top-level menu array â€” one entry per UI module, ordered by actor priority (core modules first), with the module's entryPoint route
navigation.menuVisible must be true for pages reachable directly from the menu, false for pages only reachable via in-page flows

subNav is optional â€” include only when a module has 3 or more peer sections that warrant a persistent tab bar (e.g. HR Admin with Leave Types / Balances / Requests tabs); omit for simple linear workflows
navigation.flows covers in-page transitions only â€” menu-level navigation is covered by the catalog menu array
